changes_summary: v3 commits to concrete single-option decisions for every v2 flag. Discovery drops the 60-day window (anti-join + reaction threshold are sufficient; any qualifying OP imports regardless of age, fixing FLAG-001/correctness-1). Promoter uses `WHERE EXISTS` prefilter on member existence plus a parallel `system_logs` insert naming skipped rows, so a single FK violation no longer aborts the sync (fixes FLAG-006/correctness-2). The Cloudflare duration promise is dropped — the existing `cloudflare-stream-webhook` only writes HLS URL + thumbnail + storage_provider per lines 210-240, and v3's contract matches that exactly (fixes FLAG-005/all_locations-1). The vault bootstrap is replaced with a self-contained `internal.secrets` table + SECURITY DEFINER accessor migration that applies cleanly on `supabase db reset` without any pre-existing secret; the operator seeds the key via a documented one-line INSERT at provisioning time (fixes all_locations-2). The "existing lightbox" promise for comment media is downgraded to `<a href={media.url} target="_blank">` tiles; a real lightbox is explicitly flagged as follow-up (fixes FLAG-007/scope/callers). The PL/pgSQL pivot from the brief's two-edge-function shape is promoted to an explicit settled decision in the Overview with rationale (addresses issue_hints). Adds a concrete regexp validation step and a missing-member regression test to the Validation Order.

flags_addressed.reason: Dropped the `created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '60 days'` filter entirely. Discovery now relies solely on the anti-join (via ON CONFLICT on discord_thread_id) + reaction threshold, so OPs of any age that reach 5 reactions import on the next tick. Regression test added.
Same fix as FLAG-001; late-threshold coverage now has no temporal boundary.

Cloudflare duration dropped from the promise (webhook at lines 210-240 only writes HLS URL, thumbnail, storage_provider). Vault pattern replaced with a self-contained `internal.secrets` table + SECURITY DEFINER accessor that applies cleanly on supabase db reset.
v3 does not promise duration anywhere; success criteria updated accordingly. If duration is later required, a one-line webhook change is noted as a follow-up.

Replaced `supabase_vault`-based bootstrap with a postgres-owned `internal.secrets` table + accessor function. Migration is fully self-contained (no out-of-band `current_setting` dependency) and applies cleanly on `supabase db reset`. Operator seeds the key via a one-line INSERT documented in the migration header.

Promoter now uses `WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM members WHERE member_id = m.author_id)` prefilter. Skipped rows are explicitly logged to `system_logs` in a separate INSERT. A single FK-missing author can no longer abort the sync.
Same fix as correctness-2: EXISTS prefilter + system_logs sweep. A regression test (manually delete a referenced member, rerun) is added to the Validation Order.

Removed the 'existing lightbox' promise. Comment media tiles and 'Made with this' tiles are now `<a href={media.url} target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">` wrappers around the thumbnails. A real lightbox is documented as a follow-up task.
Same fix as FLAG-007: v1 ships with browser-native new-tab navigation, no new modal/state surface.
Same fix as FLAG-007: new-tab anchors require no existing caller boundary.

Overview now explicitly documents the PL/pgSQL pivot (and cursor-table removal) as a settled design decision with rationale: matches repo's existing cron pattern, removes edge-function auth dependency, and the anti-join covers exactly what the cursor was meant to do. Listed in Overview settled decisions.
Already addressed in v2 (workflow/lora mapping). Preserved in v3.
Already addressed in v2 (cron uses internal helper, not current_setting). Preserved; v3 tightens the helper to use internal.secrets instead of supabase_vault.

questions: Step 1 audit: exact literal for manual-upload `media.classification` (for OP imports) — the existing value we reuse, and whether the field is a CHECK list or a Postgres ENUM.

Step 1 / Step 11 audit: the exact column `cloudflare-stream-webhook` uses to look up `media` rows — Stream UID in which named column? Our importer must insert with that same column populated so the webhook finds the row.
Step 5: is `internal` an acceptable schema name to introduce, or should I use `discord_sync` / some existing schema already in the repo?
Step 10: does the site already have a Supabase Storage bucket named `user-uploads`, or should Discord imports use a different bucket / path prefix?
Step 19: does `ResourceDetail` already render a 'Made with this' section for manual assets that we need to merge with, or is this entirely new (the Discord-only gated section)?
Step 19: is hard-coding the guild ID (one constant shared across all 5 channels) acceptable, or should we store it per-channel — e.g. pulling from `discord_channels.guild_id` if that table exists?

Step 16: does `PostBodyRenderer` allow raw HTML pass-through or strip it? We inherit its posture exactly; the answer decides whether Discord content with accidental HTML-like text renders as code or as HTML.

success_criteria.criterion: All Phase 1 migrations apply cleanly via `supabase db reset` on a scratch branch, including `internal.secrets` with no pre-existing value
`assets` gains source, discord_thread_id, discord_channel_id, imported_at, last_synced_at, reactions_reached_threshold_at, is_hidden plus unique partial index on discord_thread_id
`asset_comments`, `asset_comment_media` (with is_deleted), `media_import_jobs` exist with specified columns, indexes, and FKs to `members(member_id)`
`asset_media` gains is_deleted BOOLEAN DEFAULT FALSE

RLS enabled on all new tables; asset_comments / asset_comment_media have public-read SELECT filtered by is_deleted=false; media_import_jobs and internal.secrets have no policies (service-role / postgres only)

`internal.secrets` is postgres-owned with RLS enabled; `internal.get_service_role_key()` is SECURITY DEFINER with EXECUTE granted only to postgres; migration applies cleanly without a pre-seeded secret
`internal.discord_promote_resources()` PL/pgSQL function exists, is SECURITY DEFINER, and is callable via pg_cron without HTTP
Promoter discovery scans `discord_messages` WITHOUT any `created_at` time window; any qualifying OP of any age imports on the next tick (regression test for FLAG-001)
A previously-unreactable OP older than 60 days that is manually updated to `reaction_count=5` is imported by the next promoter tick

Promoter uses `WHERE EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM members ...)` prefilter on both asset insertion and comment insertion, so a row with a missing `author_id` FK is skipped (logged to system_logs) without aborting the function
With a manually-deleted referenced `members` row, the promoter completes successfully, logs the skipped OP to `system_logs` with component='discord_resource_promoter', and imports all other OPs
Promoter maps channel 1149372684220768367 -> type='workflow' and the other four channels -> type='lora'
Running the promoter a second time produces zero net new rows in assets, asset_comments, or media_import_jobs
`regexp_match(url, '/attachments/\d+/(\d+)/')` correctly extracts the attachment ID from a real Discord CDN URL containing `?ex=…&is=…&hm=…` query parameters
A Discord edit that removes an attachment results in the corresponding `asset_comment_media`/`asset_media` row being marked `is_deleted=TRUE` on the next promoter tick
A Discord message edit updates `asset_comments.content` and `discord_edited_at`; a Discord deletion sets `asset_comments.is_deleted=true`

Media importer, given one seeded job, creates a `media` row (with Stream UID for video; with public Storage URL for image), creates the matching junction row, and marks the job `status='done'` with `media_id` set

For a video attachment: the importer does NOT poll Cloudflare; the existing `cloudflare-stream-webhook` populates `cloudflare_playback_hls_url` and `cloudflare_thumbnail_url` when Cloudflare signals ready (duration is intentionally not promised)
Rehosted video URLs return a valid HLS playlist (HTTP 200 with `#EXTM3U` in body); rehosted image URLs return HTTP 200 with an image content-type
A simulated failed job increments attempts, records last_error, is re-queued with growing locked_until, and moves to status='failed' after 5 attempts

pg_cron has two schedules: `discord-resource-promoter` (*/10, direct SQL call to `internal.discord_promote_resources()`) and `discord-media-importer` (*/2, `net.http_post` with Authorization via `internal.get_service_role_key()`)
`useResources.ts`, `useCommunityResources.ts`, `useCommunityResource.ts`, `useUserProfile.ts` all SELECT source, discord_channel_id, is_hidden and filter is_hidden=false

A shared `AssetDescription` component (`banodoco-website/src/components/resources/AssetDescription.tsx`) renders asset.description using react-markdown + remark-gfm mirroring `PostBodyRenderer.tsx`, with NO custom embed tokens (::art[uuid], etc.)
`ResourceDetail/index.tsx:166-169` renders `asset.description` via `AssetDescription` for both manual and Discord-imported assets
`ResourceModal.tsx:186-189` renders `asset.description` via `AssetDescription`
ResourceDetail shows 'Made with this' grid, 'Discussion' thread, and 'From Discord' badge only when `resource.source === 'discord_import'`; manual assets render unchanged

Comment media tiles and 'Made with this' tiles are anchor tags with `target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer"` pointing at the rehosted media URL; clicking opens the asset in a new tab (no in-app lightbox in v1)

Each rendered comment shows author avatar + name from `members`, markdown-rendered content via `AssetDescription`, reply chip when applicable, attachment tiles, reaction count chip, and a working 'View on Discord' link
`useAssetComments` fetches the joined data in a single Supabase query, filters is_deleted=false on both `asset_comments` and `asset_comment_media`, and orders by `discord_created_at` ascending
Setting `assets.is_hidden=true` on an imported asset makes it disappear from all four hook-backed surfaces and from `ResourceDetail` accessed by URL
Existing website tests and Supabase type generation still pass after schema additions
Resource cards show a subtle 'From Discord' badge with the channel friendly name for imported assets
Media importer wall-clock stays under 400s per invocation; promoter (SQL) finishes in under 10s per run
Health-check SQL snippets return usable output (stuck jobs, 24h failures, staleness) against live tables
Rollback + follow-up doc exists and explicitly names the deferred items (comment lightbox, Cloudflare duration, supabase_vault migration)
No Cloudflare Stream quota / storage cost surprises during backfill

assumptions: Channel-to-type mapping is `resources` (1149372684220768367) -> `workflow`; all four model-family channels -> `lora`. Matches the website's `lora | workflow` enum at `SubmitResource/index.tsx:10-13`.

Archive pipeline populates `members` before writing a `discord_messages` row that references the author. When a rare race occurs, the promoter's EXISTS prefilter skips that OP and logs it; it imports on the next tick after the archive catches up.

`internal.secrets` is the right owner-restricted pattern for the service-role key. RLS is enabled with no policies; only postgres (and service_role, which bypasses RLS) can read. Operator seeds the value via one-time INSERT post-deploy.
`cloudflare-stream-webhook/index.ts:210-240` writes `cloudflare_playback_hls_url`, `cloudflare_thumbnail_url`, and `storage_provider`. It does NOT persist duration; v3 honors that contract exactly.

Existing pg_cron usage (`20260403000005_schedule_priority_scores_cron.sql`) schedules direct SQL. Our promoter follows that pattern; only the media importer needs HTTP, authenticated via `internal.get_service_role_key()`.

The PL/pgSQL pivot from the brief's two-edge-function + cursor-table shape is an endorsed simplification: the anti-join on `assets.discord_thread_id IS NULL` fully replaces the cursor's role, and SQL-native execution removes the 30s wall-clock risk and edge-function auth dependency.

Parsing Discord attachment ID from the CDN URL path (`/attachments/{channel}/{attachment}/{filename}`) is reliable; the regex `/attachments/\d+/(\d+)/` matches before any query string, and `regexp_match` returns NULL (not NO DATA FOUND) on miss.
All 5 `_resources` channels live in one guild; the guild ID is a hard-coded frontend constant for 'View on Discord' links.
`members.username` is NOT NULL per `20251101000000_create_discord_tables.sql:38-64`; FKs target `members(member_id)`; no thin-member fallback is attempted.

`react-markdown` + `remark-gfm` are already website dependencies (used by `PostBodyRenderer`). `AssetDescription` inherits the sanitization posture of `PostBodyRenderer` minus the custom embed tokens.
`admin_status='Listed'` is a valid value in the existing `assets.admin_status` enum/CHECK.
OP attachments use the existing manual-upload `media.classification` value captured in Step 1. Reply attachments use the new `'discord-comment'` value.

`reaction_count >= 5` against `discord_messages.reaction_count` is the correct threshold signal; the `sync_message_reactors` trigger keeps this denormalized total current per `20260319100000_fix_sync_reactors_trigger.sql:1-22`.
Universal markdown rendering of `assets.description` (manual + imported) is safe because the `AssetDescription` component inherits `PostBodyRenderer`'s sanitization config; no new attack surface.

Comment media tiles clicking through to the rehosted Cloudflare/Storage URL in a new tab is acceptable v1 UX; a real in-app lightbox is deferred to a follow-up and explicitly tracked in the rollback/risk doc.
